---
title: "The Document Viewer Toolbar"
---

The Document Viewer toolbar is located at the bottom of the Document Viewer. This toolbar enables you to adjust the display magnification of each document page, rotate a page, and also navigate between document pages using the following commands.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Toolbar Element | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Displays the first page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Displays the previous page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
| `<Current page>/<Total number of pages>` | Type the number of the page where you want to navigate. |
|  | Displays the next page in document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Displays the last page in the document. This button is enabled if the current document has more than one page. |
|  | Zooms out. You can also press Alt and -. |
|  | Zooms in. You can also press Alt and +. |
|  | Rotates the image to the left. You can also press Ctrl + Left. |
|  | Rotates the image to the right. You can also press Ctrl + Right. |
|  | Fits the current image to the width or height of the Document Viewer area so that maximum enlargement is possible. You can also press Alt + B. |
|  | Fits the current image to the height of the Document Viewer area. You can also press Alt + H. |
|  | Fits the current image to the width of the Document Viewer area. You can also press Alt + W. |
